lifecycle.note: A polished product page rendered from one model.

blocks.title: A calm product page that still carries structured launch state
blocks.lede: Use Dossier as a product microsite, feature brief, or launch packet. The page reads like a public launch note, but agents can still inspect the embedded model, claims, and release gates.

blocks.caption: A product or venue page can use generated or inlined imagery and still remain a portable single file.

cards.title: Public-facing
cards.body: Hero, feature cards, social proof, FAQ, and a restrained visual system.

cards.title: Agent-readable
cards.body: Release gates, source-backed claims, and next steps stay embedded for follow-up work.

cards.title: Easy to host
cards.body: Drop the generated HTML into GitHub Pages, a docs site, or an iframe.

blocks.title: Launch shape
blocks.subtitle: A product page can mix human copy with operational launch state.

steps.title: Scan
steps.body: Understand the product promise from the hero and cards.

steps.title: Compare
steps.body: Use tables and FAQs to answer adoption questions.

steps.title: Trust
steps.body: Review source-backed claims and readiness gates.

steps.title: Act
steps.body: Send the generated page to users, reviewers, or agents.

blocks.title: Feature snapshot
rows: Sales, support, and partners can open the artifact anywhere.
Agents can inspect the same source humans approved.
Claims stay tied to sources and evidence.
Readers can download Markdown, JSON, or a digest.

blocks.title: Launch claims
blocks.summary: Claims that appear in the page can be grounded in sources and evidence.

sources.label: Local build
sources.summary: The example was generated with the Dossier CLI.

sources.label: Embedded model
sources.summary: The source model ships inside the generated HTML.

claims.claim: The launch page is a self-contained HTML artifact.
Agents can recover the structured model without DOM scraping.

items.q: Can this replace a marketing site?
items.a: Use it for focused launches, product briefs, internal enablement, or campaign pages. Keep a full CMS for long-lived marketing operations.

items.q: Can the same model drive follow-up work?
items.a: Yes. Agents can read the model, trust report, release gates, and notes through export packets or MCP.
